phrases.translation: Physical persons and living communities with real interests at stake
phrases.legalRef: UDHR Art. 1 — Right to Dignity / UDHR Art. 21 — Right to Participation

phrases.translation: Civilian casualties — physical bodies of non-combatants killed or wounded
phrases.legalRef: Geneva Convention AP I, Art. 51 — Protection of the Civilian Population

phrases.translation: Shift in energy production — physical communities in fossil fuel regions face livelihood displacement without guaranteed replacement
phrases.legalRef: UDHR Art. 23 — Right to Work & Dignity / Planetary Boundaries

phrases.translation: Emissions target allowing continued release balanced by offsets — does not prevent ongoing thermal stress to habitat and physical communities
phrases.legalRef: Planetary Boundaries Framework — Thermal Threshold / Ecocide Framework

phrases.translation: Targeted physical destruction or killing framed as accurate to reduce perceived moral weight
Sustained aerial destruction of physical infrastructure and persons
phrases.legalRef: Geneva Convention AP I, Art. 51 — Prohibition of Indiscriminate Attack

phrases.translation: Restriction of trade and financial access — physical impact includes civilian shortages of food, medicine, and fuel
phrases.legalRef: Geneva Convention IV, Art. 55 / UDHR Art. 25

phrases.translation: Insufficient access to food for physical survival — a condition caused by policy, conflict, or resource extraction, not natural scarcity
phrases.legalRef: Geneva Convention IV, Art. 55 / UDHR Art. 25 — Right to Food

phrases.translation: Deployment of machines to replace physical workers — displacing persons from livelihoods with no guaranteed transition support
phrases.legalRef: UDHR Art. 23 — Right to Work & Dignity

phrases.translation: Physical human deaths and injuries — persons killed or wounded in conflict
Physical human beings reduced to numerical abstractions
phrases.legalRef: UDHR Art. 1 — Right to Human Dignity

phrases.translation: Physical harms borne by bodies and habitats that accounting systems treat as invisible
Acknowledgment that shifting away from fossil fuels has physical costs for workers and communities — the 'just' qualifier remains aspirational without binding commitments
phrases.legalRef: UDHR Art. 23 — Right to Work & Dignity / Planetary Boundaries Framework

phrases.translation: NATO bombing campaign causing civilian casualties and infrastructure destruction
phrases.legalRef: UN Charter Article 2(4) prohibition on use of force; potential violations of proportionality under IHL

phrases.translation: Political euphemism for the mass forced removal of people — including citizens — on ethnic or cultural grounds; a direct habitat displacement programme
phrases.legalRef: Rome Statute Art. 7 — Deportation / Forcible Transfer as Crime Against Humanity

phrases.translation: intensified military strikes causing civilian casualties and infrastructure destruction
People lacking access to sufficient safe drinking water or irrigation water for food production, described as a technical metric rather than a crisis of access.
